comments.author: @gigiessam4567
comments.text: Women in Egypt can divorce men as well ,she gave him the dowery back and she is free as bird

replies.author: @ESO529-w5i
replies.text:
@reallifeincountries For all I know and hear: yes. Such info are wide-spread - the problem is that women often don't have much (financial) means to support themselves. Add to this the factor that the norm is to have children as soon as circumstances allow. It's a proof of "manhood" to have a pregnant wife asap after the wedding is consumated.

It doesn't apply though to the Coptic Egyptian society, which is roughly +/- 15% ... except that there too the wife gets pregnant as fast as possible ًںک†. They have to be legally separated for 5 yrs, then get Church consultation and try to mend things for another 5 years and appear again in front of the clergy; then they're granted the OK to go ahead with the divorce procedure which can easily take another 3-4 yrs. This is why women sometimes convert to Islam ًںک‰ and then they're divorced instantly. Although religiously mixed marriages are not legally an issue and are allowed, provided the man is Muslim since the father is responsible for the kids education (at least formally). These are the info I have from our Coptic friends. I hope this helps to further clarify the issue.

replies.author: @reallifeincountries
replies.text: Spotlight on legal facts! âڑ–ï¸ڈ ًں“œ Thank you both for elaborating on the 'Khul' practiceâ€”it adds crucial nuance to the cultural understanding. Do you think this legal right is widely understood and accessible across all regions today?"

replies.author: @ESO529-w5i
replies.text: It's called "Khul" and any woman can obtain it without the consent of the husband. It's an  informal/no-fault divorce. A woman can legally end her marriage through the Egyptian Family Court by officially stating she can no longer  - or doesn't want to - continue living with her husband. Before granting the divorce, the court usually orders a reconciliation and arbitration period to attempt to save the marriage. And it goes without saying that the woman also gives up her right of alimonies.

replies.author: @user-g8l6i
replies.text:
The history of 
Cleopatra was born in Alexandria, Egypt, in 69 BC. While she was an Egyptian queen and the last active ruler of the Ptolemaic Kingdom, her family was of Macedonian Greek origin. Her lineage traced back to Ptolemy I, one of Alexander the Great's generals who established the ruling dynasty in 323 BC.

comments.text: Good video. You say that the Grand Museum put Cairo on the map...Cairo was on the map when half of Europe's aristocracy vacationed in Egypt from the 1910s to the early 1950s. The crux of this presence was in the 30s and 40s. Egypt has always been on the map for that matter.

It just goes to show again that it has nothing to do with religions but everything with traditions. Also in view of the fact that the absolute majority of Muslim countries do not practice FGM although they have no specific laws forbidding it.

replies.author: @LarrySchwall-q3h
replies.text: â€‹@reallifeincountriesthat may be a little difficult to understand as well as explain. Throughout my life I have been driven or guided to places that I had no desire to go to. But once I arrived there I was glad for the experience that is greater than reading something in a book. In Egypt my life is far better than most people in America fresh fruits and vegetables daily have very low rent, electric bill $6.00 a month with air-conditioning. A very, very safe place to live, day or night no matter where you are. The list of benefits of living here are vastly different from living in America on every level.

comments.author: @ashrefelkashef9263
comments.text: Why distort the truth, poison the well, and try to portray modern Egyptians as a backward, donkey-riding, and impoverished group, contrary to reality? Horses, camels, and donkeys, Mr. Publisher of this report, are all for leisure in the desert near the pyramids, and koshari and feseekh are equivalent to sausages in our country. For your information, they are a very cultured and extremely generous people, and there is no discrimination between white and black people. They are a respectable people, and I respect the integrity of their respectable class, who are the majority because they have existed for 35,000 years. They are a nation, an army, and civilizations. Their only fault is that throughout history, everyone coveted what the Egyptians possessed. Some came with armies to fight them, and others came as refugees seeking their mercy. When they became the majority, they turned against the Egyptians and occupied their land.

replies.author: @user-g8l6i
replies.text: Cleopatra was born in Alexandria, Egypt, in 69 BC. While she was an Egyptian queen and the last active ruler of the Ptolemaic Kingdom, her family was of Macedonian Greek origin. Her lineage traced back to Ptolemy I, one of Alexander the Great's generals who established the ruling dynasty in 323 BC.

comments.text: The contrast is absolutely wild. Millions travel to Egypt to marvel at the ancient wealth of the Pharaohs, while completely walking past the real, modern survival of the people living right next door. Thank you for showing the human side of Egypt that the brochures hide
